rationale: The citing paper uses Iyer and Villas-Boas (2003) in its core discussion of why estimated manufacturer and retailer conduct parameters may deviate from the benchmark double-marginalization model. It specifically invokes the cited paper's nonspecifiability and renegotiation logic as a concrete channel explanation for lower inferred marginal wholesale pricing and retailer bargaining effects. Although this is not the main structural model estimated in the paper, the citation plays a meaningful interpretive role rather than a generic literature-list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the cited bargaining-theory mechanism (product nonspecifiability and ex post renegotiation) to interpret its empirical channel-pricing results in ketchup. It uses that framework as one substantive explanation for observed deviations from standard Nash/coordination benchmark margins.
